zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Talley於1685年12月21日出生在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓時，他的父親Richard Talley34歲，母親Sarah Blake29歲。他於1720年與Mary Lightfoot Tucker在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 喬治普林斯 喬治結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1764年在美國肯塔基州勞雷爾享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Talley于1685年12月21日出生在美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓时，他的父亲Richard Talley34岁，母亲Sarah Blake29岁。他于1720年与Mary Lightfoot Tucker在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 喬治普林斯 喬治结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1764年在美国肯塔基州勞雷爾享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Talley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Tolley  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of the places in northern France named Tailly (Somme, Ardennes). It is uncertain that this surname survived into modern times.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Taithligh ‘descendant of Taithleach’, a byname or personal name meaning ‘peaceable’. This surname is now very rare in Ireland.

variants.name: Tally
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Talley  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tallie
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Talley  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland. In the US, it is found mainly among African Americans.

variants.name: Talty
meanings: Irish (Clare): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tailtigh ‘descendant of Tailteach’, a personal name which Woulfe believes to be a variant of Taithleach, a byname or personal name meaning ‘peaceable’ (see Talley ).

variants.name: Tolley
meanings: English (mainly West Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Toli (Old Norse Tóli, a particularly East Scandinavian pet form of names in Thórr ‘the god Thor’ + an element beginning with l-).
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Russian Telyatev: nickname or metonymic occupational name derived from telyata ‘calves’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Talley was born on 21 December 1685, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States, his father, Richard Talley, was 34 and his mother, Sarah Blake, was 29. He married Mary Lightfoot Tucker in 1720, in Prince George, Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1764, in Laurel, Kentucky, United States, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Talley est né le 21 décembre 1685, à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, États-Unis, son père, Richard Talley, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Sarah Blake, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Mary Lightfoot Tucker en 1720, à Prince George, Prince George, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1764, à Laurel, Kentucky, États-Unis, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Talley am 21. Dezember 1685 in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Talley 34 und seine Mutter Sarah Blake 29. Er hat 1720 in Prince George, Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America Mary Lightfoot Tucker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1764 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Laurel, Kentucky, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Talley è nato il il 21 dicembre 1685 nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Richard Talley, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Sarah Blake, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Mary Lightfoot Tucker nel 1720, nel Prince George, Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1764, nel Laurel, Kentucky, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Talleyが1685年12月21日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンで生まれた時，父Richard Talleyは34歳，母Sarah Blakeは29歳であった。1720年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州プリンス ジョーッジプリンス ジョーッジでMary Lightfoot Tuckerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1764年にアメリカ合衆国ケンタッキー州ラウレルで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Talley(이)가 1685년 12월 21일 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Talley(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Blake(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1720년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프린스 조지 프린스 조지에서 Mary Lightfoot Tucker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1764년 미국 켄터키 주 로럴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Talley nasceu em 21 de dezembro de 1685, em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Richard Talley, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Blake, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Lightfoot Tucker em 1720, em Prince George, Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1764, em Laurel, Kentucky, Estados Unidos da América, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Talley родился 21 декабря 1685 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Richard Talley было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Blake, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Lightfoot Tucker в 1720 г. в местности принц джеордж, принц джеордж, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1764 г. в местности лоурел, Кентукки, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Talley nació el 21 de diciembre de 1685, en Boston, Suffolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, su papá, Richard Talley, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Sarah Blake, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Mary Lightfoot Tucker en 1720, en Prince George, Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1764, en Laurel, Kentucky, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 79 años.
